cases.name: W3S Ticketing
cases.comment: Benchmarks the W3S Web3 Summit ticketing dapp (https://github.com/paritytech/tick3t apps/w3s/contracts/w3s). The W3S contract is an ERC721 NFT ticket built on OpenZeppelin v5.6.1 (ERC721 + AccessControl) with per-token encrypted attendee names, role-based admin (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE / W3F / VOLUNTEER), and native-token payment. Setup deploys W3S, grants W3F to DEPLOYER, allocates 10 volunteers with VOLUNTEER role, and pre-whitelists 750 FREE_USER_K accounts via DEPLOYER.addFreeTicketBatch. The mint loop runs 750 iterations, each minting three tickets via all three paths: buyTicket, buyTicketFor, and redeemFreeTicket (from FREE_USER_K, already whitelisted). 750 × 3 = 2250 tickets total. Then 10 volunteers each check in a 150-token shard covering tokens 1..1500; tokens 1501..2250 are left unchecked (realistic no-shows). The W3S.sol in this workload is the forge-flattened output of src/W3S.sol with openzeppelin-contracts@v5.6.1 inlined; to refresh, re-run `forge flatten src/W3S.sol` against a fresh OZ install.

inputs.comment: Allocate the deployer (gets DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE in the constructor).
Deploy W3S with a 1 wei ticket price and a dummy x25519 public key (bytes32). The deploying account receives DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE.
DEPLOYER (DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE) grants W3F to itself so it can manage the whitelist via addFreeTicketBatch and grant VOLUNTEER role.

Allocate 10 volunteer accounts and grant each the VOLUNTEER role. Pre-watcher because volunteer onboarding isn't part of the door-side load we're measuring. Each outer-K iter creates VOLUNTEER_K (via $VARIABLE: composition on the target name) and DEPLOYER grants the role. consolidate_state propagates VOLUNTEER_0..9 to the parent scope so the post-watcher check-in block can address them.

Pre-whitelist 750 free-ticket users. Each outer-K iter allocates FREE_USER_K and DEPLOYER whitelists it via addFreeTicketBatch (ABI: offset=0x20, length=1, single padded address). Pre-watcher because whitelisting isn't part of the door-side load we're measuring — and doing it here avoids needing await_transaction_inclusion in the mint loop. consolidate_state propagates FREE_USER_0..749 to the parent scope so the mint loop can address them by composition.

Mint loop: 750 iterations, each minting three tickets via all three paths (buyTicket, buyTicketFor, redeemFreeTicket). FREE_USER_K is pre-allocated and pre-whitelisted in the previous block, so the iter just submits its redeemFreeTicket directly. Total mints = 2250. start_watcher: true so metrics begin at the mint loop. No await_transaction_inclusion needed because allocate_account dispenses pre-funded keys with no on-chain tx, and each within-iter call uses a fresh sender with no nonce dependency.

Volunteer-sharded check-in. Outer K=0..9 picks VOLUNTEER_K (via $VARIABLE: composition on the caller), inner I=0..149 walks the per-shard token-id range I + K*150 + 1. Result: VOLUNTEER_0 → tokens 1..150, ..., VOLUNTEER_9 → tokens 1351..1500. Token 1501 (the redeemed free ticket) is left unchecked — a realistic no-show.
